questions.question: Which icon do you click at the top of the Notion Mail sidebar to start composing a new email?

options.explanation: 📎 is used for attaching files, not for starting a new email.
📝 is the compose button shown at the top of the Notion Mail sidebar.
🗑️ discards a draft; it does not start a new email.
📅 inserts your availability, not a new email.

questions.question: What icon do you use in the composer to attach a file to your email?

options.explanation: 📎 is the attachment icon; clicking it opens a file picker.
📝 opens the composer, not the attachment dialog.
📅 inserts your calendar availability, not a file.
There is no link icon in the composer for attachments.

questions.question: Which of the following correctly describes the steps to create a new snippet from the composer?

options.text: Click {} at the bottom → Select New snippet → Set a shortcut → Enter content → Save
options.explanation: These are the exact steps listed for creating a snippet.

options.text: Click {} at the bottom → Select Turn into snippet → Choose a shortcut → Save
options.explanation: That sequence describes turning a draft into a snippet, not creating a new one.

options.text: Type /snippet in the body → Enter content → Press Enter → Save
options.explanation: Snippets are created via the {} menu, not by typing a slash command.

options.text: Click 📎 → Upload a file → Choose "Make snippet" → Save
options.explanation: 📎 is for attachments; it does not create snippets.

questions.question: Quick replies are generated only for which type of email threads?

options.text: Newsletters
options.explanation: The article states quick replies are NOT generated for newsletters.

options.text: Invoices
options.explanation: Invoices are also excluded from quick replies.

options.text: Conversations with other people
options.explanation: Quick replies appear for normal email conversations with other people.

options.text: Automated system alerts
options.explanation: System alerts are not mentioned as generating quick replies.

questions.question: How can you schedule an email to be sent at a later date and time?

options.text: Click the 📎 icon and select "Schedule"
options.explanation: 📎 is for attachments; it does not schedule sending.

options.text: Select the "v" next to Send at the bottom of the composer and choose date, time, and time zone
options.explanation: The "v" next to Send opens the scheduling UI.

options.text: Type /schedule in the body of the email
options.explanation: Scheduling is not triggered via a slash command.

options.text: Turn the draft into a snippet and set a future send date
options.explanation: Snippets store content; they do not schedule sending.

questions.question: What does typing "/" inside the email body do?

options.text: It opens a menu of block types you can insert into the email
options.explanation: The slash menu provides blocks such as headings, images, etc.

options.text: It immediately sends the email
options.explanation: Sending requires clicking Send; the slash does not send.

options.text: It creates a new snippet automatically
options.explanation: Snippets are created via the {} menu, not the slash command.

options.text: It opens the quick replies panel
options.explanation: Quick replies appear at the bottom of an open thread, not via a slash.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT listed as a possible cause of email delivery failure?

options.text: Gmail network timeout
options.explanation: This is listed as a possible cause.

options.text: Gmail rate limit error
options.explanation: This is also listed.

options.text: Incorrect recipient email address format
options.explanation: The article does not mention address formatting as a cause; it lists token revocation, internal error, etc.

options.text: Gmail token revoked
options.explanation: A revoked token is specifically mentioned as a cause.

questions.question: When turning an email draft into a snippet, which elements can you edit before saving?

options.text: Only the shortcut name
options.explanation: You can edit more than just the shortcut name.

options.text: Content, subject, recipient, and shortcut name
options.explanation: The article states you can edit all of these fields.

options.text: Only the email body content
options.explanation: Subject and recipient are also editable.

options.text: Only the recipient and subject
options.explanation: The content itself is also editable.

questions.question: Which icon do you click to insert your Notion Calendar availability into an email?

options.explanation: 📎 attaches files, not calendar availability.
📅 is the icon for inserting availability from Notion Calendar.
🗑️ discards drafts.
📝 opens the composer; it does not insert calendar data.

questions.question: How do you discard (delete) an email draft you are working on?

options.text: Click the 🗑️ icon at the bottom of the composer
options.explanation: 🗑️ is the delete‑draft button.

options.text: Press the Delete key on your keyboard
options.explanation: The article specifies using the 🗑️ icon, not a keyboard shortcut.

options.text: Select "Turn into snippet" and then cancel
options.explanation: Turning into a snippet does not delete the draft.

options.text: Click the 📎 icon and choose "Remove"
options.explanation: 📎 is for attachments; it does not delete drafts.
